name: TRUGS
description: TRUGS specification — protocol, language, validator, 7 domain examples, academic paper

folder_structure.description: Top-level repo structure — spec, language, examples, tools, paper

research.description: Long-form research studies authored against the TRUGS spec

properties.name: TRUGS
properties.purpose: TRUGS v1.0.0 specification — protocol (7 boundaries, 16 rules), language (190 words, BNF grammar), validator, examples, paper

properties.name: README.md
properties.purpose: Overview — what TRUGS is, specification index, tool reference, link to TRUGS-AGENT

properties.name: LICENSE
properties.purpose: Apache 2.0 — TRUGS LLC

properties.name: AGENT.md
properties.purpose: Agent instructions — TRL vocabulary primer, repo navigation, validation commands

properties.name: NOTICE
properties.purpose: Apache 2.0 attribution notice — TRUGS LLC copyright and license

properties.name: TRUGS_PROTOCOL
properties.purpose: The TRUGS specification — 7 boundaries, 10 primitive classes, 16 validation rules, domain branches, JSON schema

properties.name: TRUGS_PROTOCOL/CORE.md
properties.purpose: The universal foundation — 7 boundaries, 10 semantic primitive classes (~140 primitives), composition rules, 16 validation rules

properties.name: TRUGS_PROTOCOL/BRANCHES.md
properties.purpose: Domain-specific vocabularies — Python, Web, Writer, Knowledge, Living, Nested, Research branches with node types and edge relations

properties.name: TRUGS_PROTOCOL/SCHEMA.md
properties.purpose: JSON schema reference — formal field definitions, types, constraints for nodes and edges

properties.name: TRUGS_PROTOCOL/SPEC_fundamentals.md
properties.purpose: Gentle introduction to CORE concepts — minimal valid TRUG, required fields, hierarchy, dimensions

properties.name: TRUGS_PROTOCOL/SPEC_validation.md
properties.purpose: All 16 validation rules with implementations — 9 structural (always), 7 compositional (core_v1.0.0)

properties.name: TRUGS_LANGUAGE
properties.purpose: TRL specification — 190-word vocabulary, BNF grammar, 30 parsed examples, self-defining TRUG

properties.name: TRUGS_LANGUAGE/SPEC_vocabulary.md
properties.purpose: 190 words across 8 parts of speech — every word defined with source (computation or law) and CORE status

properties.name: TRUGS_LANGUAGE/SPEC_grammar.md
properties.purpose: BNF grammar, composition rules, 12 validation rules for TRL sentences

properties.name: TRUGS_LANGUAGE/SPEC_examples.md
properties.purpose: 30 parsed examples across 13 patterns — each sentence decomposed into subject, modal, verb, object

properties.name: TRUGS_LANGUAGE/language.trug.json
properties.purpose: The opening TRUG — the language defining itself as a graph

properties.name: TRUGS_LANGUAGE/README.md
properties.purpose: Language overview — what TRL is, how sentences compile to graphs

properties.name: EXAMPLES
properties.purpose: 7 domain examples — knowledge, living, memory, nested, research, web, writer — all validate against CORE

properties.name: tools
properties.purpose: Reference toolchain — validator (16 rules), memory (remember/recall/forget), CRUD (tget, tupdate, tdelete, tunlink)

properties.name: tools/validate.py
properties.purpose: TRUGS validator — 16 CORE rules, CLI interface, batch mode

properties.name: tools/test_validate.py
properties.purpose: Validator test suite — 47 tests covering all 16 rules plus integration

properties.name: tools/memory.py
properties.purpose: LLM memory as a TRUG graph — remember, recall, forget, associate commands

properties.name: tools/trl.py
properties.purpose: TRUG/L compiler — deterministic TRUG/L ↔ TRUG round-trip (tokenize, parse, compile, decompile, validate)
properties.trl: PROCESS compiler SHALL MAP STRING DATA source TO RECORD graph.

properties.name: tools/memory_render.py
properties.purpose: Deterministic markdown render of memory TRUG graphs with budget enforcement
properties.trl: PROCESS render SHALL MAP RECORD graph TO STRING DATA output.

properties.name: tools/memory_audit.py
properties.purpose: Memory hygiene — dead-rule detection, hit tracking, reconcile candidates
properties.trl: PROCESS audit SHALL FILTER ALL RECORD memory THEN RETURN RECORD dead_rule.

properties.name: tools/memory_import.py
properties.purpose: Bulk import flat markdown files with YAML frontmatter into a memory TRUG graph
properties.trl: PROCESS importer SHALL READ ALL FILE markdown THEN WRITE EACH RECORD memory TO RECORD graph.

properties.name: tools/build_language_trug.py
properties.purpose: Parse SPEC_vocabulary.md into language.trug.json — 190 words, 242 nodes
properties.trl: PROCESS builder SHALL MAP ALL RECORD word TO RECORD graph.

properties.name: tools/tget.py
properties.purpose: Read a node by ID — JSON or formatted output, optional edge listing

properties.name: tools/tupdate.py
properties.purpose: Update a node's properties by ID

properties.name: tools/tdelete.py
properties.purpose: Delete a node and all its edges by ID

properties.name: tools/tunlink.py
properties.purpose: Remove an edge between two nodes

properties.name: PAPER
properties.purpose: Academic paper — TRUGS specification, TRL compilation algorithm, graph-sentence equivalence

properties.name: REFERENCE
properties.purpose: Canonical home for TRUGS papers, standards, and cross-cutting material — the document trinity: WHY, HOW, CHECK

properties.name: REFERENCE/README.md
properties.purpose: Index of the REFERENCE library — trinity overview, layout, cross-links

properties.name: REFERENCE/PAPER_dark_code.md
properties.purpose: The WHY paper — Dark Code problem and TRUGS four-corner verification square as the resolution. Moved from TRUGS-AGENT per #1548.
properties.trl: PROCESS paper SHALL DEFINE RECORD dark_code THEN SHALL CITE A RECORD solution.

properties.name: REFERENCE/PAPER_how_to_code_with_trugs.md
properties.purpose: The HOW paper — practitioner guide: greenfield process, refactoring, multi-file, AAA integration, memory, CI, patterns, Python conventions
properties.trl: PROCESS paper SHALL DEFINE RECORD practice THEN CITE A RECORD workflow.

properties.name: REFERENCE/STANDARD_dark_code_compliance.md
properties.purpose: The CHECK — auditable Dark Code compliance checklist. Operationalizes the four corners into C1-C7 mechanical checks.
properties.trl: PROCESS standard SHALL ADMINISTER EACH RESOURCE repo SUBJECT_TO DATA compliance.

properties.name: tools/compliance_check.py
properties.purpose: Mechanical Dark Code compliance verifier — implements C1-C7 from STANDARD_dark_code_compliance.md. Entrypoint: trugs-compliance-check.
properties.trl: PROCESS compliance SHALL VALIDATE ALL FILE SUBJECT_TO DATA standard THEN RETURN RECORD report.

properties.name: STUDIES
properties.purpose: Long-form research studies authored against the TRUGS spec — current contents: STUDY_001 (medical evidence synthesis) and STUDY_002 (executable semantic graph genealogy).

properties.name: pyproject.toml
properties.purpose: Python package metadata: name=trugs, version=1.2.0, console scripts (trugs-validate, trugs-memory, trugs-memory-render, trugs-memory-import, trugs-memory-audit, etc.), zero runtime dependencies.

properties.name: CHANGELOG.md
properties.purpose: Keep-a-Changelog format — all notable changes to TRUGS

properties.name: PLAN_1544_byte_identical_round_trip.md
properties.purpose: Work-product plan for #1544 byte-identical round-trip

properties.name: PLAN_1544_pr_b_formatting.md
properties.purpose: Work-product plan for #1544 PR-B formatting

properties.name: CONTRIBUTING.md
properties.purpose: Contribution guide — branch naming, AAA protocol, compliance gates, HITM rule

properties.name: SECURITY.md
properties.purpose: Security policy — private vulnerability disclosure via GitHub Security Advisories

properties.scope: CORE defines the universal foundation all other specs build on
Domain vocabularies built on CORE structure
JSON schema formalizing CORE field definitions
Self-defining TRUG uses vocabulary words as nodes
All examples validate against CORE rules
Python implementation of all 16 validation rules
STANDARD operationalizes the four-corner square defined in the PAPER
HOW paper derives from §5 of the Dark Code paper
CLI mechanically enforces C1-C7 from the STANDARD
Delegates C7 to trugs-folder-check via subprocess
